what_it_does: Implements a variable-resolution engine that turns a declarative kind (from MS_KINDS) into a concrete instance by resolving each variable against project context using a precedence ladder: explicit, preference, memory, AI, default. It also resolves reference variables to existing artifacts, records why a value was chosen, gathers soft influence signals for generation, and biases/selects variants based on learned feedback.
observations: Resolution precedence is explicit > preference > memory > ai > default.
A slot's `source` sets the lowest-authority tier it will use, and every higher tier plus default is still considered.
`tier` returns `{has, value}` so falsey values like 0, '', false are treated as valid resolutions.
Memory resolution for constrained variables only accepts recalled content that matches one of the declared options.
Reference variables (`type:'ref'`) always resolve: either to a bound artifact id/name or to a label describing the referenced kind/family.
`explain` produces a full precedence trace for transparency UIs.
`influences` collects recalled memories and curated prefer/avoid signals scoped to the kind's category.
`variantBias` deterministically picks a variant index, preferring values named in 'prefer' signals and avoiding 'avoid' signals.
`noteVariant` writes a prefer/avoid learning when a variant is approved/rejected, closing the learning loop.
The module depends on global window registries: MS_KINDS, MS_MEMORY, MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_FEEDBACK, MS_VARY.
standouts: Reference variables never fail loudly; they bind to the most recent approved artifact or fall back to a type label.
The separate 'influence' pass surfaces soft shaping signals without overriding strict variable resolution.
The variant approval/rejection mechanism maps a decision directly onto an axis value that future `variantBias` calls read.
`consideredTiers` uses the slot's `source` as a floor, not a single tier, which is a subtle but important design choice.
conventions: Uses an IIFE attached to `window` as a namespace module.
Relies on global singleton registries rather than imports or parameters.
Defensive null/undefined checks before accessing registry methods.
Deterministic, unit-testable helper functions with no side effects except `noteVariant`.
concerns: The regex for matching memory content against options is rebuilt on every call inside a loop.
Unconstrained memory variables use arbitrary recalled text as the resolved value, which may be noisy.
`refBind` selects 'approved first, then most recent' without any explicit relevance scoring.
The `0.12` memory-score threshold in `influences` is a magic constant.
`preference` tier mixes `ctx.preferences` and `window.MS_MEMORY.preference` without documenting precedence between them in the code.
No validation that `ctx` or `slot` have expected shapes.
notes: This file is the binding layer between templates and project reality: it decides what each variable becomes, explains why, and feeds variant decisions back into future generation.
questions: What exact object shape does `MS_MEMORY.recall` return, and what does `score` represent?
How is `slot.source` assigned in practice, and what values are valid besides the ORDER names?
What is the exact structure of `MS_VARY[kindId]` — is it always `[axisKey, [values]]`?
How is `slot.memoryTopic` intended to be used, given it is passed as `topic` to `recall`?
Does `MS_MEMORY.preference(slot.key)` return a project-level or account-level preference?
How is relevance determined when multiple artifacts match a ref category?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it resolves kind variables by precedence, binds refs, exposes a trace, and connects learning signals to variant selection.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make a single universal kind template produce many project-specific instances while keeping every resolution decision inspectable, and to let past user approvals quietly shape future defaults without hard-coding them into the kind.
novelty: The distinct separation between strict variable resolution and a soft 'influence' pass, plus the bidirectional variant learning loop where approving a variant writes a scoped prefer/avoid signal that the same resolver later reads to bias defaults.
contribution: A deterministic, precedence-driven resolver with explainability, reference binding to project artifacts, and a feedback-driven variant bias mechanism that a fused system could keep as its variable-binding and learning-loop component.
summary_for_embedding: A JavaScript variable-resolution engine that binds kind template variables to concrete values using explicit, preference, memory, AI, and default tiers, resolves references to project artifacts, and provides a learning-to-generation feedback loop via variant bias and influence signals.
intention_for_embedding: To turn declarative kind definitions into project-specific instances while keeping resolution transparent and letting past approvals shape future variant selection.

symbols.does: Attempts to resolve a variable from one named precedence tier.
symbols.description: Takes a tier name, slot definition, and context, then returns `{has, value}` so that legitimately falsey values are still counted as resolved. It implements the lookup logic for explicit, preference, memory, AI, and default tiers.

symbols.does: Computes which precedence tiers a slot will consult.
symbols.description: Uses the slot's `source` as the lowest-authority tier and includes every higher tier in ORDER plus default, so a 'memory' slot still honors explicit and preference values.

symbols.does: Finds an existing artifact matching a referenced kind or category.
symbols.description: Filters `MS_ARTIFACTS` by kind id or category, prefers approved artifacts, and returns the most recent match as `{id, name}`; returns null if nothing matches.

symbols.does: Returns a human-readable label for a referenced kind or category.
symbols.description: Looks up the kind label or category label from `MS_KINDS`, falling back to a capitalized version of the reference id so a ref always has a descriptive name.

symbols.does: Resolves a single slot into a binding record.
symbols.description: Walks the considered tiers, returns the first resolved value with its source, handles ref binding specially so refs never fail, and marks required variables with no default as unresolved while logging an error.

symbols.does: Resolves all variables of a kind into a full instance record.
symbols.description: Loads the kind, resolves each variable, collects bindings and values, lists unresolved keys, and attaches influence signals for the kind.

symbols.does: Maps a kind's category to a feedback scope.
symbols.description: Returns 'synthesis' for style and motion categories and 'generation' for everything else, aligning with the feedback vocabulary used elsewhere.

symbols.does: Collects soft signals that should shape generation of a kind.
symbols.description: Returns the feedback scope plus recalled memories for the kind and curated prefer/avoid signals, degrading to empty arrays if the memory or feedback registries are absent.

symbols.does: Deterministically selects a variant index biased by learned preferences.
symbols.description: Reads the kind's variant axis from `MS_VARY`, prefers an index whose value is named in `prefer` signals and not in `avoid` signals, and falls back to the first non-avoided or index 0.

symbols.does: Tests whether any text in a list mentions a value as a whole word.
symbols.description: A small helper used by `variantBias` to check if prefer/avoid signal texts contain a given axis value using a case-insensitive word-boundary regex.

symbols.does: Returns the variant axis definition for a kind.
symbols.description: Looks up `MS_VARY[kindId]` and returns `{key, values}` if present, otherwise null.

symbols.does: Records a prefer/avoid learning from an approved or rejected variant.
symbols.description: Maps a variant index to its axis value and writes a scoped learning via `MS_FEEDBACK.addLearning`, returning the axis, value, and scope; returns null if the kind has no axis.

symbols.does: Produces a full precedence trace for a slot.
symbols.description: Returns an ordered array showing every tier, whether it was considered, whether it had a value, the value if any, and which tier was ultimately chosen, for transparency UIs.
